task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Rate-Limited API** (Level 7)

Build any CRUD API with rate limiting. POST /api/items creates items (name required). GET /api/items lists all. Apply rate limiting: max 5 requests per minute. The 6th request within a minute should return 429.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Items table:
  name, required

# Your endpoints here
# Hint: rate limit 5 per minute

```

The app must pass these HTTP tests:
  - POST /api/items with body {"name
final_source:
build for javascript backend

database is local memory

create a Items table:
  name, required

create a RateLimits table:
  ip_address, required
  request_count, default 0
  minute_start_time, default 0

define function check_rate_limit():
  limit_key = 'global_limit'
  existing = look up RateLimits with limit_key
  
  if existing is empty:
    new_record = save { ip_address: limit_key, request_count: 1, minute_start_time: 0 } to RateLimits
    send back false
  
  set existing's request_count to (existing's request_count + 1)
  save existing to RateLimits
  
  if existing's request_count is greater than 5:
    send back true
  
  send back false

when user calls POST /api/items sending entry:
  is_rate_limited = check_rate_limit()
  
  if is_rate_limited is true:
    send back 'Rate limit exceeded' with status 429
  
  validate entry:
    name is text, required
  
  saved = save entry to Items
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/items:
  is_rate_limited = check_rate_limit()
  
  if is_rate_limited is true:
    send back 'Rate limit exceeded' with status 429
  
  items = get all Items
  send back items
